title: Dainty Little Love
content:
Dainty little Love came tripping
         Down the hill,
     Smiling as he thought of sipping
         Sweets at will.
            SHE said, "No,
            Love must go."
     Dainty little Love came tripping
         Down the hill.

     Dainty little Love went sighing
         Up the hill,
     All his little hopes were dying - 
         Love was ill.
            Vain he tried
            Tears to hide.
     Dainty little Love went sighing
         Up the hill.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Arthur Macy, titled "Dainty Little Love", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
